paper_title: Honey and Pollen Analysis in M. scutellaris Colonies

methods.description: Process for collecting honey samples from M. scutellaris colonies to ensure samples represent honey produced each month.

steps.description: Honey was collected monthly from selected honey pots.
steps.searchable_summary: collect monthly honey samples from selected pots

steps.description: Pots were observed biweekly and compared with a picture of the colony taken during the previous harvest.
steps.searchable_summary: compare biweekly observations with previous harvest images

steps.description: A 50-mL honey sample was collected from each colony using a disposable syringe.
Samples were stored in individual tubes.

methods.description: Comparison of pollen types in honey samples with those in flowering plants and reference slides.

steps.description: Qualitative analysis of pollen spectra involved comparison of the pollen types present in the honey samples and those present in the flowering plants in the study area.
steps.searchable_summary: compare pollen types in honey with flowering plants

steps.description: The pollen types were also compared with pollen identified in reference slides from the Pollen Collection at UFRB and descriptions in specialized literature.
steps.searchable_summary: compare pollen types with reference slides and literature

methods.description: Quantitative analysis of pollen in honey samples to determine relative frequency and classification of pollen types.

steps.description: All honey samples collected in a given month were pooled.
Slides were prepared from the samples of honey collected based on a method by Erdtman (1960).
steps.searchable_summary: prepare slides from pooled honey samples

steps.description: The slides were visualized using an optical microscope and photographed; 1000 pollen grains were counted for each sample.
steps.searchable_summary: count and photograph pollen grains using microscope

steps.description: The relative frequency of each pollen type was established using the formula: f = (ni N -1 ) × 100.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate relative frequency of pollen types

steps.description: Pollen types were classified according to frequency: dominant pollen (> 45% of total grains), accessory pollen (16 -45%), important isolated pollen (3 -15%), and occasional isolated pollen (< 3%).
steps.searchable_summary: classify pollen types based on frequency
